github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text:
Generate a SINGLE commit message summarizing ALL changes together. Use conventional commit format: <type>: <description>. Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert. Keep subject line under 72 characters. Focus on WHY the change was made and WHAT changed overall. Do NOT break down into multiple commits or list individual changes separately. Combine multiple related changes into one concise message that captures the overall purpose of the commit.

## Naming conventions

- Branch: `{description}` when starting from main/master/default.
- Commit: `{type}: {description}` (terse), where `{type}` is one of: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `build`, `ci`, `chore`, `revert`
- PR title: `{description}` summarizing the full diff.

## Commit type selection rules

- Use `fix` when behavior for users/systems was wrong and is now corrected.
- Use `refactor` when code structure changes but behavior is intentionally unchanged.
- Use `feat` for net-new user-facing or developer-facing capability.
- Use `perf` only when the primary purpose is measurable performance improvement.
- Use `docs` for documentation-only changes.
- Use `style` for formatting/style-only edits with zero behavior impact.
- Use `test` for tests-only additions/updates.
- Use `build` for dependency/build tooling updates.
- Use `ci` for pipeline/workflow automation changes.
- Use `chore` for maintenance work that does not fit above categories.
- Use `revert` only when reverting a prior commit.
